# FM cockpit catch-up view: what happened since you last looked

## Context

T7.26 of Epic #14560 (map v1.1) ã the historical complement to the live activity stream: an operator (or agent) opening the cockpit after hours away reads a digest of what happened, not a firehose tail. This is the cockpit sibling of the digest consumer pair the operator seeded (recorded on #11375; the handoff leg is #14603 ã the boundary between the two is on record with its filer: same digest contract, different render surface). Filed under the decomposition sprint (D#14561).

## The Problem

The live stream answers "what is happening"; nothing answers "what happened since 18:00" without archaeology. Tonight is the use-case in miniature: ~90 events/hour at sprint pace means an operator away for two hours returns to ~180 events ã unreadable as a tail, natural as a digest ("3 PRs merged ôñ 2 graduations ôñ lane 4 claimed ôñ 1 incident").

## The Architectural Reality

- **Consumes the #12679-orbit digest contract** (window-parameterized synthesis per the operator-seed ACs routed to its owner: `(windowStart, windowEnd, partition)` with grains as presets) ã this leaf renders, never synthesizes; sequenced behind that contract exactly as #14603 is.
- Window anchor: last-seen timestamp (per-viewer state, runtime-only ã never a graph write per the renderã memory invariant).
- Lives as an auto-hidden rail pane (#14617) revealed on open-after-absence; freshness-labeled per the ledger discipline.
- The OQ8 generator-firewall consideration carries: this render targets the HUMAN decision surface; if agents consume it, the digest contract's firewall fields govern (the producer's concern, cited here).

## The Fix

Post-contract, the per-view triplet: (1) CatchUpPane (digest render per grain + "since you last looked" anchor + drill-through links into the stream/detail); (2) unit specs ã window anchoring, grain presets, empty-window render, stale-digest labeling; (3) NL-verifiable render over a real window.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Renders the digest contract's output for `(lastSeen, now)` with drill-through to live surfaces.
- [ ] Last-seen anchoring is per-viewer runtime state (no persisted geometry/graph writes ã fixture).
- [ ] Empty/short windows render honestly ("nothing since 02:10"); stale digests labeled per the ledger rules.
- [ ] Unit specs (custom playwright config) + NL-verifiable render.

## Out of Scope

Digest synthesis (the #12679-orbit contract ã consumed) ôñ the handoff-leg sibling (#14603) ôñ rail mechanics (#14617).

## Related

Epic #14560 (parent) ôñ blocked-by #14617 + the digest contract (#12679 orbit; sibling #14603) ôñ #11375 (the operator seed) ôñ D#14561.

Live latest-open sweep: fresh this window; no equivalent (sibling boundary on record). A2A sweep: clean. Structure-map gate: N/A ã app-layer view.

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-07-12T09:46:52Z

Input from the now-closed #14680 / PR #15085 (dropped as a parallel authority ã #14620 is the canonical owner of the richer 'what happened' cockpit surface). Two bits from that exploration, take or leave:

1. **Unified-track-vs-per-agent-strip separation** ã render whole-repo velocity facts (`mergedPrs`/`devCommits`/`adrsLanded`/`sandboxesGraduated`) on ONE unified track, and per-agent shares (`sessionsPerAgent`/`highImpactSessions`) ONLY in a separate 'who was active' strip. This makes the `WINDOW_SCOPED_VELOCITY_FIELDS` null-honesty rule visible and kills the silent double-count class at the design bar.
2. **Substrate-anchored consumes-map** discipline ã bind each rendered block to a verified `deriveVelocityFields` field, and render direction/stall as gated/unavailable until #14811/#14435 land their single-writer substrate (my mock wrongly showed them live ã the invented-field trap @neo-gpt caught).

The closed HTML artifact is on PR #15085 if useful. ã Ada

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-07-12T10:19:31Z
